summary: Tested WebSocket notification system for LEAMSS Portal. Backend WebSocket implementation is CORRECT and working locally. External WebSocket connections fail due to Kubernetes ingress not supporting WebSocket upgrade requests. Notification system has a working fallback via REST API polling.

critical.issue: WebSocket connections fail through external URL (wss://immi-hub-1.preview.emergentagent.com/ws/{token}). Error: 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'. This is an INFRASTRUCTURE issue - Kubernetes ingress needs WebSocket upgrade support configuration.
critical.root_cause: Kubernetes ingress/load balancer not configured to support WebSocket upgrade requests
critical.workaround: Notification system falls back to REST API polling every 60 seconds

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket connection fails on external URL, but frontend correctly handles this with reconnection attempts and REST API polling fallback

local_websocket_tests.connection_with_valid_token: PASSED - WebSocket connects successfully on localhost:8001
local_websocket_tests.notification_broadcast: PASSED - When ticket is created, targeted user receives real-time WebSocket notification
local_websocket_tests.invalid_token_rejection: PASSED - Connection rejected with invalid JWT token

external_websocket_tests.reason: Kubernetes ingress does not support WebSocket upgrade

frontend_verification.notification_dropdown: Working - Shows notifications loaded via REST API

action_items: INFRASTRUCTURE: Configure Kubernetes ingress to support WebSocket connections. This typically requires adding annotations like 'nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: 3600' and 'nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-send-timeout: 3600' and ensuring WebSocket upgrade headers are passed through.
ALTERNATIVE: If ingress WebSocket support cannot be enabled, consider using Server-Sent Events (SSE) as an alternative for real-time notifications, which works over standard HTTP.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend WebSocket implementation is correct - ConnectionManager, ping/pong, and notification broadcast all work locally
Frontend NotificationBell.jsx correctly implements WebSocket client with reconnection logic and REST API polling fallback
The 60-second polling interval provides a reasonable fallback when WebSocket is unavailable

success_rate.frontend: 90% - UI works, WebSocket connection fails due to infrastructure

seed_data_creation: Created test tickets with prefix 'TEST_WS_Notification_' for WebSocket testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket notification system code is correct and works locally. The issue is INFRASTRUCTURE - Kubernetes ingress needs to be configured to support WebSocket upgrade requests. The frontend has a working fallback mechanism (REST API polling every 60 seconds) so notifications still work, just not in real-time. To verify WebSocket works, test locally with ws://localhost:8001/ws/{token}.
